content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA, we can do the following operation as many times as needed :


Choose (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤∣A∣1 \le i < j \le |A|1≤i<j≤∣A∣ and Ai=AjA_i = A_jAi​=Aj​, then delete both the values from the array, and append 2⋅Ai2 \cdot A_i2⋅Ai​ at the end of the array.
For example, suppose A=[3,1,3,4,4]A = [3, 1, 3, 4, 4]A=[3,1,3,4,4], and we choose (i,j)=(1,3)(i, j) = (1, 3)(i,j)=(1,3), then the new array formed is [1,4,4,6][1, 4, 4, 6][1,4,4,6].

Let f(A)f(A)f(A) denote the minimum possible size of the array AAA after doing these operations.

Given an array AAA of size NNN, solve for all prefixes of the array AAA. More formally, find the values f(A[1,1],f(A[1,2]),f(A[1,3]),....,f(A[1,N])f(A[1, 1], f(A[1, 2]), f(A[1, 3]), ...., f(A[1, N])f(A[1,1],f(A[1,2]),f(A[1,3]),....,f(A[1,N]), where A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] denotes the array [AL,AL+1,...,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, ..., A_R][AL​,AL+1​,...,AR​].


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the size of the array
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the values f(A[1,1],f(A[1,2]),f(A[1,3]),....,f(A[1,N])f(A[1, 1], f(A[1, 2]), f(A[1, 3]), ...., f(A[1, N])f(A[1,1],f(A[1,2]),f(A[1,3]),....,f(A[1,N])
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : We show the solutions for first 333 prefixes

[4][4][4] : No operation can be performed, answer is 111
[4,2][4, 2][4,2] : No operation can be performed, answer is 222
[4,2,2][4, 2, 2][4,2,2] : First use i=2,j=3i = 2, j = 3i=2,j=3 operation to transform the array to [4,4][4, 4][4,4]. Now i=1,j=2i = 1, j = 2i=1,j=2 operation to get [8][8][8].
